title: Unbounded memory growth in readEventLedger / rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded
description: Both functions load the entire events.jsonl into memory via readFile and split by newline. For a 50MB ledger (the soft-warning threshold) this instantiates millions of strings simultaneously, causing heavy GC pressure and potential OOM under concurrent sessions or large corpora.
fix_direction: Migrate readEventLedger and rotateEventLedgerIfNeeded to readline.createInterface + fs.createReadStream for O(1)-memory linear parsing.

title: O(N) full-corpus in-memory file scans during search
description: searchEntries performs a linear O(N) scan across all pending and canonical trees, calling readFile on every markdown file to parse frontmatter for titles/summaries/tags. This O(N) FS + parsing load degrades search latency severely as the KB grows 10x-100x.
fix_direction: Implement a pre-computed description_index or SQLite backing store to serve search queries without blocking on thousands of file reads per request.

title: Linear directory scans for session hint hygiene
description: Session hints create per-session cache files (.fabric/.cache/session-hints-*.json). inspectSessionHintsStale scans the cache directory linearly to purge stale sessions. With concurrent multi-window sessions on the same repo these files accrete rapidly, making readdirSync progressively slower.
fix_direction: Consolidate session hint state into a unified LRU cache file or lightweight store, or shard by day to bound directory listings.

title: Hardcoded directory structures impede multi-store scaling
description: Multiple services (review, extract-knowledge, doctor lints) hardcode iterations over static paths like .fabric/knowledge and ~/.fabric/knowledge. As the multi-store git model (~/.fabric/stores/<id>) expands, these static loops fail to discover cross-store knowledge dynamically or incur heavy latency enumerating cloned stores.
fix_direction: Abstract knowledge paths into a dynamic store registry/workspace locator that resolves ~/.fabric/stores/<id> and queries only relevant indexed stores.

title: Hardcoded default top_k silently truncates matches
description: The retrieval pipeline ranks candidates and truncates with a default plan_context_top_k limit. As corpus size scales 10x-100x, highly relevant entries are silently omitted if they rank below the static count threshold, causing retrieval starvation on large KBs.
fix_direction: Adopt a dynamic top_k that scales with corpus size, or rely on a BM25/vector score minimum threshold instead of an absolute scalar cap.
